author: Dr. Lizard
title: What are the diagnostic criteria for MDS-SF3B1 in WHO-5 versus ICC classifications?
answer: Both require SF3B1 mutation with low blasts and exclude del(5q), monosomy 7, and complex karyotype.
detail: Low blast threshold is less than 2% in peripheral blood and less than 5% in bone marrow.
WHO-5 also allows diagnosis with 15% or more ring sideroblasts even without SF3B1 mutation present.
ICC requires SF3B1 mutation for this category; ring sideroblasts without mutation become MDS NOS.
Both systems exclude cases with del(5q), monosomy 7, or complex karyotype from the SF3B1 category.
releaseNote: The diagnostic criteria for MDS-SF3B1 in WHO-5 and ICC classifications require SF3B1 mutation with low blasts and exclude specific cytogenetic abnormalities.
